title: mc
text: devtools::document() rebuild NAMESPACE man pages devtools::test() run tests pkgdown::build_site() docs site Follow tidyverse/r-lib conventions (roxygen2 markdown, testthat v3)

title: mc
text: Standards bookdown report projects across New Graph Environment.

title: mc
text: canonical references. Child repos inherit structure patterns. mybookdown-template — General-purpose bookdown starter fish_passage_template_reporting — Fish passage reporting template doubt, match template . template production repos disagree, production wins — update template.

title: mc
code: project/ ├── index.Rmd                # Master config, YAML params, setup chunks ├── _bookdown.yml            # book_filename, output_dir: "docs" ├── _output.yml              # Gitbook, pagedown, pdf_book config ├── 0100-intro.Rmd           # Chapter numbering: 4-digit, 100s increment ├── 0200-background.Rmd ├── 0300-methods.Rmd ├── 0400-results.Rmd ├── 0500-*.Rmd               # Discussion/recommendations ├── 0800-appendix-*.Rmd      # Appendices (site-specific in fish passage) ├── 2000-references.Rmd      # Auto-generated from .bib ├── 2090-report-change-log.Rmd  # Auto-generated from NEWS.md ├── 2100-session-info.Rmd    # Reproducibility ├── NEWS.md                  # Changelog (semantic versioning) ├── scripts/ │   ├── packages.R           # Package loading (renv-managed) │   ├── functions.R          # Project-specific functions │   ├── staticimports.R      # Auto-generated from staticimports pkg │   ├── setup_docs.R         # Build helper │   └── run.R                # Local build (gitbook + PDF) ├── fig/                     # Figures (organized by chapter or type) ├── data/                    # Project data ├── docs/                    # Rendered output (GitHub Pages) ├── renv.lock                # Locked dependencies └── .Rprofile                # Activates renv

title: mc
text: Every index.Rmd follows setup sequence. Order matters. Responsive settings output format:
code: # 1. Gitbook vs PDF switch gitbook_on <- TRUE  # 2. Knitr options knitr::opts_chunk$set(   echo = identical(gitbook_on, TRUE),  # Show code only in gitbook   message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,   dpi = 60, out.width = "100%" ) options(scipen = 999) options(knitr.kable.NA = '--') options(knitr.kable.NAN = '--')  # 3. Source in order: packages → static imports → functions → data source('scripts/packages.R') source('scripts/staticimports.R') source('scripts/functions.R') # Gitbook photo_width <- "100%"; font_set <- 11  # PDF (paged.js) photo_width <- "80%"; font_set <- 9

title: mc
text: Parameters live index.Rmd frontmatter (separate file). Child repos override editing values. Fish passage repos add project-specific params (project_region, model_species, wsg_code, update flags forms). project-specific — don’t add general template.
code: params:   repo_url: 'https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/repo_name'   report_url: 'https://www.newgraphenvironment.com/repo_name/'   update_packages: FALSE   update_bib: TRUE   gitbook_on: TRUE

title: mc
text: Embed context purpose chunk names. principle universal; codes project-specific. Pattern: {type}-{system}-{description}

title: mc
text: Bookdown auto-prepends fig: tab: chunk names. Tables: Table \@ref(tab:chunk-name) Figures: Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name) fig: tab: prefix chunk label — bookdown adds .

title: mc
text: Interactive tables (DT) can’t use standard bookdown captions. Use my_tab_caption() function staticimports.R. Pattern: Separate -cap chunk table chunk. my_tab_caption() auto-grabs chunk label via knitr::opts_current$get()$label wraps HTML caption tags bookdown can cross-reference.
code: # Caption chunk — must use results="asis" {r tab-sites-sum-cap, results="asis"} my_caption <- "Summary of fish passage assessment procedures." my_tab_caption() # Table chunk — renders the DT {r tab-sites-sum} data |> my_dt_table(page_length = 20, cols_freeze_left = 0)

title: mc
text: Separate prep chunk (find file) display chunk (render ).
code: # Prep — find the photo {r photo-196332-01-prep} my_photo1 <- fpr::fpr_photo_pull_by_str(str_to_pull = 'ds_typical_1_') my_caption1 <- paste0('Typical habitat downstream of PSCIS crossing ', my_site, '.') # Gitbook — full width {r photo-196332-01, fig.cap=my_caption1, out.width=photo_width, eval=gitbook_on} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) # PDF — side by side with 1% spacer {r photo-196332-d01, fig.show="hold", out.width=c("49.5%","1%","49.5%"), eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) knitr::include_graphics("fig/pixel.png") knitr::include_graphics(my_photo2)

title: mc
text: references.bib auto-generated — never edit manually. build, rbbt::bbt_write_bib() scans .Rmd files @citekey references, pulls BibTeX Zotero’s Better BibTeX, overwrites references.bib. manual additions lost next build. add reference: add shared Zotero group library, use BBT citation key (@key) .Rmd text, build. rbbt handles rest. update_bib: FALSE params, build uses existing references.bib without regenerating — useful offline builds CI Zotero isn’t running. Auto-generate package citations: Use nocite: YAML include references cited text.
code: bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" biblio-style: apalike link-citations: no knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), 'bookdown', 'knitr', 'rmarkdown'), 'packages.bib')

title: mc
text: index.Rmd contains two separate front-matter sections setup chunks:

title: mc
text: Three parts, order: Personal connection land (template-level, across reports): > New Graph Environment, understand well-inseparable health land waters work within. care ecosystems, care communities connected . relationship metaphorical — foundation practice. Colonial acknowledgement (template-level): > Modern civilization long journey ahead acknowledge address historic ongoing impacts colonialism… Territorial acknowledgement (project-specific, must edited per report): Name Nations, governance systems, watersheds, species relevant project. use generic office-location acknowledgement — tie territory work happens. See Wedzin Kwa chinook example pattern. Funding partners (project-specific).

title: mc
text: use # heading disclosure — creates separate chapter page gitbook. Instead, add YAML date: field renders title block: Wording principle: accurate LLM . assisted drafting synthesis — make scientific interpretations conclusions. say “independently verified authors” (redundant) attribute “ecological assessments” LLM. regulatory/EGBC-stamped work, use extended disclaimer soul/research/20260212_ai_disclosure_research.md. See NewGraphEnvironment/mybookdown-template#89.
code: date: |  |  | Version X.X.X DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")`  |  | *Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic) assisted with literature synthesis, drafting, and technical writing. All scientific interpretation, data analysis, and conclusions are the responsibility of the authors.*

title: mc
text: single boolean gitbook_on controls output format throughout.
code: # Show only in gitbook {r map-interactive, eval=gitbook_on}  # Show only in PDF {r fig-print-only, eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)}  # Conditional inline content `r if(identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)) knitr::asis_output("This report is available online...")`  # Page breaks for PDF only `r if(gitbook_on){knitr::asis_output("")} else knitr::asis_output("\\pagebreak")`

title: mc
text: Reports use MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH versioning NEWS.md changelog. Version index.Rmd YAML: NEWS.md format: Auto-append appendix via my_news_to_appendix() staticimports.R: Convention: - Bump version index.Rmd add NEWS entry every commit main changes report content - Tag releases: git tag -v1.1.0 -m "v1.1.0: Brief description" - MAJOR: structural changes, new chapters, methodology changes - MINOR: new content, figures, tables, discussion sections - PATCH: prose fixes, corrections, formatting
code: date: |  |  | Version 1.1.0 DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")` ## 1.1.0 (2026-02-17)  - Add feature X - Fix issue Y ([Issue #N](https://github.com/Org/repo/issues/N)) news_to_appendix(md_name = "NEWS.md", rmd_name = "2090-report-change-log.Rmd")

title: mc
text: Always use ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog() — never hardcode viewer iframes. function includes cache-busting ?v= parameter. Bump v function default viewer.html breaking changes.
code: knitr::asis_output(ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog("https://bucket.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/ortho.tif"))

title: mc
text: Use renv reproducible package management: - .Rprofile activates renv startup - renv::restore() installs lockfile - renv::snapshot() updates lockfile adding packages - Use pak::pak("pkg") install (install.packages)

title: mc
text: Production repos (2024-2025) drifted templates areas. working child repo, match repo , template: Script naming 02_reporting/ — older repos use tables.R, 0165-read-sqlite.R; newer repos use numbered 0130-tables.R. Follow repo ’re . Removed packages — elevatr, rayshader, arrow removed production still template. staticimports::import() call — repos skip source staticimports.R directly. Hardcoded vs parameterized years — older repos hardcode years file paths; newer repos use params$project_year. Prefer parameterized.

title: mc
text: Use gq package shared layer symbology. Never hardcode hex color values registry style exists. Core pattern: reg$layers$lake, reg$layers$road, reg$layers$bec_zone, etc.
code: library(gq) reg <- gq_reg_main()  # load once per script — 51+ layers

title: mc
text: project-specific layers main registry, use hand-curated CSV merge: Install: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/gq")
code: reg <- gq_reg_merge(gq_reg_main(), gq_reg_read_csv("path/to/custom.csv"))

title: mc
text: sf_use_s2(FALSE) top every mapping script Compute area simplify SQL map title — title belongs report caption Legend least-important terrain — swap legend logo sides reduces AOI occlusion. fixed convention side. Four-corner rule — legend, logo, scale bar, keymap get corner. Never stack two quadrant. Bbox must match canvas aspect ratio — compute ratio geographic extents page dimensions. Mismatch causes white space bands. Consistent element--frame spacing — inset elements visually equal margins frame edge Map fills frame — basemap extends edge--edge, dead bands. Use near-zero inner.margins outer.margins. Suppress auto-legends — build manual ones registry values CAPS labels appear larger — use title case legend labels (gq gq_tmap_classes() handles automatically via to_title() fallback)

title: mc
text: Read PNG check showing anyone: Correct polygon/study area shown? (verify source data, just bbox) Map fills page? (white/black bands) Keymap inside frame spacing edge? element overlap? (corner) Legend least-important terrain? Consistent spacing across elements? Scale bar breaks appropriate extent? See cartography skill full reference: basemap blending, BC spatial data queries, label hierarchy, mapgl gotchas, worked examples.

title: mc
text: Use drift flooded together riparian land cover change analysis. flooded delineates floodplain extents DEMs stream networks; drift tracks ’s changing inside time. Pipeline: Class colors come drift’s shipped class tables (IO LULC, ESA WorldCover) production COGs S3, dft_map_interactive() serves tiles via titiler — set options(drift.titiler_url = "...") See drift vignette worked example (Neexdzii Kwa floodplain, 2017-2023)
code: # 1. Delineate floodplain AOI (flooded) valleys <- flooded::fl_valley_confine(dem, streams)  # 2. Fetch, classify, summarize (drift) rasters   <- drift::dft_stac_fetch(aoi, source = "io-lulc", years = c(2017, 2020, 2023)) classified <- drift::dft_rast_classify(rasters, source = "io-lulc") summary    <- drift::dft_rast_summarize(classified, unit = "ha")  # 3. Interactive map with layer toggle drift::dft_map_interactive(classified, aoi = aoi)

title: mc
text: Structured checklist reviewing diffs commit. Used /code-check. Add new checks bug class discovered — compound time.

title: mc
text: Variables double-quoted strings containing single quotes break value ' "echo '${VAR}'" — VAR contains ', shell syntax breaks Use printf '%s\n' "$VAR" | command pipe values safely Heredocs: unquoted <<EOF expands variables locally, <<'EOF' — know need

title: mc
text: Hardcoded absolute paths (/Users/airvine/...) break users Use REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/<relative>" && pwd)" moving scripts, verify ../ depth still resolves correctly Usage comments match actual script location

title: mc
text: || true hides real errors — failure actually safe ignore? Empty variable destructive operation (rm, destroy) — add guard: [ -n "$VAR" ] || exit 1 grep returning empty silently — downstream commands get empty input

title: mc
text: Secrets passed command-line args visible ps aux Use env files, stdin pipes, temp files chmod 600 instead

title: mc
text: Must pure ASCII — em dashes, curly quotes, arrows cause silent parse failure Check : perl -ne 'print "$.: $_" /[^\x00-\x7F]/' file.yaml

title: mc
text: cloud-init clean causes full re-provisioning next boot — almost never want snapshot Use tailscale logout tailscale snapshot (deregister vs disconnect)

title: mc
text: Secrets rendered via templatefile() readable 169.254.169.254 metadata endpoint Acceptable ephemeral machines, document tradeoff

title: mc
text: Parsing tofu state show text output fragile — use tofu output instead Missing outputs scripts need — add main.tf Snapshot/image IDs tfvars deleting snapshot — stale reference

title: mc
text: Validate resource IDs destroy: [ -n "$ID" ] || exit 1 tofu destroy without -target destroys everything including reserved IPs Snapshot ID extraction: use --resource droplet grep -F exact match

title: mc
text: .tfvars must gitignored (contains tokens, passwords) .tfvars.example variables empty/placeholder values Sensitive variables need sensitive = true variables.tf

title: mc
text: 0.0.0.0/0 SSH world-open — document intentional access gated Tailscale, say explicitly

title: mc
text: Passwords special chars (', ", $, !) break naive shell quoting printf '%q' escapes values shell safety Temp files secrets: create chmod 600, delete use

title: mc
text: pak stops first unresolvable package — subsequent packages skipped Removed CRAN packages (like leaflet.extras) must move GitHub source PPPM binaries may lag hours behind new CRAN releases

title: mc
text: Branch pins (pkg@branch) reproducible — document used Pinned download URLs (RStudio .deb) go stale — document update

title: mc
text: Moving/renaming scripts: update CLAUDE.md, READMEs, usage comments New variables: update .tfvars.example New workflows: update relevant README

title: mc
text: Standards external communications across New Graph Environment. compost working repo email drafts, scripts, contact management, Gmail utilities. conventions capture universal principles; compost implementation details.

title: mc
text: Three levels. Default casual unless context dictates otherwise. Collaborative, directive. Acknowledge constraints: Avoid: “Work makes sense lab” Better: “’re able work fits schedule really helpful”

title: mc
text: Draft markdown, convert HTML send time via gmailr. See compost script templates, OAuth setup, search_gmail.R. File naming: YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic_draft.md + YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic.R Key gotchas (documented detail compost): - Gmail strips <style> blocks — use inline styles tables - gm_create_draft() support thread_id — gm_send_message() can reply threads. Drafts land outside conversation. - Always use test_mode create_draft variables safe workflows

title: mc
text: Never manually type data tables — generate programmatically source files Link canonical sources (GitHub repos, public reports) rather embedding raw data Provide CSV Excel sharing tabular data Document ID codes — using compressed IDs (e.g., id_lab), include reference sheet recipients can decode

title: mc
text: Internal QA info (blanks, control samples, calibration data) Internal tracking codes SRED references Draft status revision history Internal project management details Keep client-facing communications focused deliverables technical content.

title: mc
text:
HTML emails, use
tags lines.
code: Al Irvine B.Sc., R.P.Bio. New Graph Environment Ltd.  Cell: 250-777-1518 Email: al@newgraphenvironment.com Website: www.newgraphenvironment.com

title: mc
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: mc
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: mc
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: mc
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: mc
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.
code: 1. [Step] → verify: [check] 2. [Step] → verify: [check] 3. [Step] → verify: [check]

title: mc
text: Core patterns professional, efficient workflows across New Graph Environment repositories.

title: mc
text: Five repos form governance operations layer across New Graph Environment work: Adaptive management: Conventions evolve real project work, theory. pattern learned refined project work, propagate back soul projects benefit. /claude-md-init skill builds project’s CLAUDE.md soul conventions. Cross-references: sred-2025-2026 tracks R&D activities across repos. Compost cross-cuts projects centralized communications workflow — email drafts, contact registry, tone guidelines live copied individual project communications/ folders needed.

title: mc
text: Check duplicates: gh issue list --state open --search "<keywords>" – search creating Link SRED: work involves infrastructure, R&D, tooling, performance benchmarking, add Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N (match repo name SRED issue title) One issue, one concern. Keep focused.

title: mc
text: Write issues clear technical focus: Use normal technical language titles descriptions Focus problem solution approach Add tracking links end (e.g., Relates Owner/repo#N) Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

what: GitHub Issue Creation - Always Use Files
title: mc
text: gh issue create command heredoc syntax fails repeatedly EOF errors. ALWAYS use --body-file:
code: cat > /tmp/issue_body.md << 'EOF' ## Problem ...  ## Proposed Solution ... EOF  gh issue create --title "Brief technical title" --body-file /tmp/issue_body.md

title: mc
text: : Close issues via commit messages. commit closure documentation. DON’T: Close issues gh issue close. breaks audit trail — ’s linked diff showing changed. Fixes #N Closes #N — auto-closes links commit issue Relates #N — partial progress, close Always close issues work complete. Don’t leave stale open issues.
code: Fix broken DEM path in loading pipeline  Update hardcoded path to use config-driven resolution.  Fixes #20 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: mc
text: Write clear, informative commit messages: commit: - Logical, atomic units work - Working state (tests pass) - Clear description changes avoid: - “WIP” “temp” commits main branch - Combining unrelated changes - Vague messages like “fixes” “updates”
code: Brief description (50 chars or less)  Detailed explanation of changes and impact.  Fixes #<issue> (or Relates to #<issue>) Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: mc
text: Rules learned real project sessions. apply across repos. Install missing packages, don’t workaround — package needed, ask user install (e.g. pak::pak("pkg")). Don’t write degraded fallback code avoid dependency. Never hardcode extractable data — coordinates, station names, metadata can pulled API database runtime, . Don’t hardcode values programmatic source. Close issues via commits, gh issue close — see Closing Issues . Cite primary sources — see references conventions.

title: mc
text: Pattern: noun_verb-detail – noun first, verb second across naming: Scripts logs live together: scripts/<module>/logs/

title: mc
text: Projects = daily cross-repo tracking (always add relevant project) Milestones = iteration boundaries (release/claim prep) Don’t double-track unless ’s reason

title: mc
text: Claude manages structured planning complex tasks using planning--files (PWF).

title: mc
text: Use PWF task multiple phases, requires research, involves ~5 tool calls. Triggers: - User says “let’s plan ”, “plan mode”, “use planning”, invokes /planning-init - Complex issue work begins (multi-step, uncertain approach) - Claude judges task warrants structured tracking Skip planning single-file edits, quick fixes, tasks obvious next steps.

title: mc
text: Explore first — Enter plan mode (read-). Read code, trace paths, understand problem proposing anything. task_plan.md — Phases checkbox tasks findings.md — Research, discoveries, technical analysis progress.md — Session log timestamps commit refs Commit plan — Commit planning files starting implementation. baseline. Work atomic commits — commit bundles code changes checkbox updates planning files. diff shows done checkbox marking done. Code check commit — Run /code-check staged diffs committing. Don’t mark task done diff passes review. Archive complete — Move planning/active/ planning/archive/ via /planning-archive. Write README.md archive directory one-paragraph outcome summary closing commit/PR ref — future sessions scan catch fast.

title: mc
text: Every commit completes planned task MUST include: - code/script changes - checkbox update task_plan.md (- [ ] -> - [x]) - progress entry progress.md meaningful creates git audit trail git log -- planning/ tells full story. commit self-documenting — can backtrack git understand everything happened.

title: mc
text: Phases checkboxes. core tracking file. Mark tasks done ’re completed: - [x] Task description
code: # Task Plan  ## Phase 1: [Name] - [ ] Task description - [ ] Another task  ## Phase 2: [Name] - [ ] Task description

title: mc
text: Append-research log. Discoveries, technical analysis, things learned.
code: # Findings  ## [Topic] [What was found, with source/date]

title: mc
text: Session entries commit references.
code: # Progress  ## Session YYYY-MM-DD - Completed: [items] - Commits: [refs] - Next: [items]

title: mc
text: planning/ doesn’t exist repo, run /planning-init first.
code: planning/   active/          <- Current work (3 PWF files)   archive/         <- Completed issues     YYYY-MM-issue-N-slug/

title: mc
text: Standards R package development across New Graph Environment repositories. Based R Packages (2e) Hadley Wickham Jenny Bryan. Reference packages: starting new package, study existing packages patterns: flooded, gq. demonstrate conventions practice (DESCRIPTION fields, README layout, NEWS.md style, pkgdown setup, test structure, hex sticker, etc.).

title: mc
text: tidyverse style guide: snake_case, pipe operators (|> %>%) Match existing patterns codebase Use pak package installation (install.packages) Prefix column name vectors cols_ discoverability environment pane: cols_all, cols_carry, cols_split, cols_writable. principle grouped vectors (params_, tbl_, etc.)

title: mc
text: Follow R Packages (2e) conventions: - R/ functions, tests/testthat/ tests, man/ docs - DESCRIPTION proper fields (Title, Description, Authors@R) - DESCRIPTION URL field: include GitHub repo pkgdown site pkgdown links correctly (e.g., URL: https://github.com/OWNER/PKG,   https://owner.github.io/PKG/) - NAMESPACE managed roxygen2 (#' @export, #' @import, #' @importFrom) - Never edit NAMESPACE man/ hand

title: mc
text: exported function gets R file test file: - R/fl_mask.R → tests/testthat/test-fl_mask.R - Commit function tests together - Use Fixes #N commit message close corresponding issue

title: mc
text: File GitHub issue function building . creates traceable record planned, built, verified.

title: mc
text: new packages major features, work branch merge via PR: gives one PR contains commits — single SRED cross-reference covers entire body work. Individual commits within branch close respective function issues Fixes #N.
code: main ← scaffold-branch (PR closes with "Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N")

title: mc
text: Close function issues via commit messages — see Closing Issues newgraph conventions.

title: mc
text: Use testthat 3e (Config/testthat/edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) Run devtools::test() committing Test files mirror source: R/utils.R -> tests/testthat/test-utils.R Test edge cases potential failures, just happy paths Tests must pass closing function’s issue Always grep errors command test run avoid running twice: error context: grep -E "(ERROR:|FAIL )" -10 | head -25
code: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(FAIL|ERROR|PASS)" | tail -5

what: Runnable examples on every exported function
title: mc
text: Examples users discover function . must: - Actually run — \dontrun{} unless external resources required - Use bundled test data via system.file() work anyone - Show function useful — just runs, produces ’d use - Use qualified names non-exported dependencies (terra::rast(), sf::st_read()) since examples run user’s environment

title: mc
text: least one vignette showing full pipeline real data: - Demonstrates package solving actual problem end--end - Uses bundled test data (committed inst/testdata/) - Hosted pkgdown users can read without installing Output format: Use bookdown::html_vignette2 (rmarkdown::html_vignette) figure numbering cross-references. Requires bookdown Suggests chunks must fig.cap numbered figures. Cross-reference Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name). Vignettes need external resources (DB, API, STAC): use .Rmd.orig pre-knit pattern — breaks bookdown figure numbering knitr evaluates chunks pre-knit emits ![](path) markdown bookdown can’t number. Instead, separate data generation presentation: 1. data-raw/vignette_data.R — runs queries, saves results .rds inst/testdata/ (inst/vignette-data/) 2. Vignette loads .rds files, chunks run live pkgdown build 3. Note top vignette: “Data generated data-raw/script.R” 4. bookdown controls chunks — figure numbers, cross-refs work pattern test data: data-raw/ documents data produced, committed artifacts make vignettes reproducible without external resource.

title: mc
text: Created via script data-raw/ documents exactly data produced (database queries, spatial crops, etc.) Committed inst/testdata/ — small enough ship package Used tests, examples, vignettes — one dataset, three purposes

title: mc
text: roxygen2 exported functions @import @importFrom package-level doc (R/<pkg>-package.R) populate NAMESPACE — don’t rely :: everywhere function bodies pkgdown site public packages _pkgdown.yml (bootstrap 5) GitHub Action pkgdown (usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown"))

title: mc
text: Run lintr::lint_package() committing R package code. Fix warnings — every lint worth fixing.

title: mc
text: 120 char line length (default 80 strict data pipelines) Allow dotted.case (common base R legacy code) Suppress commented code lints (exploratory R scripts often commented alternatives) Exclude renv directory entirely
code: linters: linters_with_defaults(     line_length_linter(120),     object_name_linter(styles = c("snake_case", "dotted.case")),     commented_code_linter = NULL   ) exclusions: list(     "renv" = list(linters = "all")   )

title: mc
text: Minimize Imports — use Suggests packages needed tests/vignettes Pin versions breaking changes known Prefer packages already tidyverse ecosystem

title: mc
text: First release: one line (e.g., “Initial release. Brief description.”) Later releases: describe changed , function--function. Link pkgdown reference page details — don’t duplicate . Don’t list every function; pkgdown reference page single source truth ’s package. Bump version DESCRIPTION (e.g., 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0) Commit “Release vX.Y.Z” Tag: git tag vX.Y.Z && git push && git push --tags

title: mc
text: Protect main deletion force pushes:
code: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/rulesets --method POST --input - <<'EOF' {   "name": "Protect main",   "target": "branch",   "enforcement": "active",   "bypass_actors": [     { "actor_id": 5, "actor_type": "RepositoryRole", "bypass_mode": "always" }   ],   "conditions": { "ref_name": { "include": ["refs/heads/main"], "exclude": [] } },   "rules": [ { "type": "deletion" }, { "type": "non_fast_forward" } ] } EOF

title: mc
text: usethis::create_package(".") usethis::use_mit_license("New Graph Environment Ltd.") usethis::use_testthat(edition = 3) usethis::use_pkgdown() usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown") usethis::use_directory("dev") — reproducible setup script usethis::use_directory("data-raw") — data generation scripts Hex sticker via hexSticker (see data-raw/make_hexsticker.R) Set GitHub Pages serve gh-pages branch

title: mc
text: Keep dev/dev.R file documents every setup step. idempotent — run interactively. reproducible recipe package scaffold.

title: mc
text: Keep README lean: - Hex sticker, one-line description, install, example showing ’s useful - Link pkgdown vignette function reference — don’t duplicate - Don’t maintain function table — ’s just another thing keep updated pkgdown’s reference page single source truth

title: mc
text: LLM assistant modifies R package code: 1. Run lintr::lint_package() — fix issues committing 2. Run devtools::test() error grep — ensure tests pass one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(FAIL|ERROR|PASS)" | tail -5 3. Run devtools::document() grep results: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::document()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(Writing|Updating|warning)" | tail -10 4. Check devtools::check() passes releases — capture results one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::check()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(ERROR|WARNING|NOTE|errors|warnings|notes)" | tail -10

title: mc
text: references flow Claude Code, Zotero, technical writing New Graph Environment.

title: mc
text: Three tools, different purposes. Use right one. Citation keys vs item keys: Citation keys (like irvine2020ParsnipRiver) come Better BibTeX. Item keys (like K7WALMSY) native Zotero. MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: mc
text: research turns reference: - DOI available: Tell user — Zotero’s magic wand (DOI lookup) fastest path - ResearchGate link: Flag user manual check — programmatic fetch blocked (403), full text often - BC gov report: Search ACAT, .gov.bc.ca library, EIRS viewer - Paywalled: Note , move . Don’t waste time trying bypass.

title: mc
text: Preferred order: 1. DOI magic wand Zotero UI (fastest, complete metadata) 2. Web API POST collections array (grey literature, local PDFs — targets collection directly, UI interaction needed) 3. saveItems via /zotero-api (batch creation structured data — requires UI collection selection) 4. JS console script group library (connector can’t target right collection) Collection targeting: saveItems drops items whatever collection selected Zotero’s UI. Always confirm user calling . Web API bypasses — include "collections": ["KEY"] POST body. Find collection keys ?q=name search collections endpoint.

title: mc
text: saveItems attachments silently fail. Don’t use . Instead: Web API S3 upload (preferred): Create attachment item → get upload auth → build S3 body (Python: prefix + file bytes + suffix) → POST S3 → register uploadKey. Works without Zotero running. See /zotero-api skill section 4. JS console fallback: Download curl, attach via item_attach_pdf.js Zotero JS console. Verify attachment exists via MCP: zotero_get_item_children

title: mc
text: manual adds, confirm via MCP: - zotero_search_items — find title - zotero_get_item_metadata — check fields complete - zotero_get_item_children — confirm PDF attached

title: mc
text: duplicates created (common saveItems retries): - Run collection_dedup.js via Zotero JS console - keeps copy attachments, trashes rest

title: mc
text: rbbt pulls BBT, syncs Zotero. Edit references Zotero → rebuild report → bibliography updates. Library targeting: rbbt must know Zotero library search. set globally ~/.Rprofile: Without option, rbbt searches personal library (libraryID 1) won’t find group library references. library IDs map Zotero’s internal numbering — use /zotero-lookup SELECT DISTINCT libraryID citationkey BBT database discover available libraries.
code: # index.Rmd — dynamic bib from Zotero via Better BibTeX bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" # default library — NewGraphEnvironment group (libraryID 9, group 4733734) options(rbbt.default.library_id = 9)

title: mc
text: [@key2020] — parenthetical: (Author 2020) @key2020 — narrative: Author (2020) [@key1; @key2] — multiple nocite: YAML — include uncited references

title: mc
text: review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. (See LLM Agent Conventions newgraph.md.) original unavailable (paywalled, print, can’t locate): use secondary citation format prose include bib entries sources: Smith et al. (2003; cited Doctor 2022) found … @smith2003 @doctor2022 go .bib file. reader can track original . Flag incomplete metadata primary entry — ’s better partial reference none .

title: mc
text: need PDF obvious URL doesn’t work: DOI resolver → publisher site (often OA link) Europe PMC (europepmc.org/backend/ptpmcrender.fcgi?accid=PMC{ID}&blobtype=pdf) — ncbi blocks curl SciELO — needs User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 header ResearchGate — flag user manual download Semantic Scholar — sometimes OA links Ask user institutional access Always verify downloads: file paper.pdf say “PDF document”, HTML.

title: mc
text: scripts/rag_build.R — maps citation keys Zotero PDF attachment keys, builds DuckDB data/rag/ gitignored — store local, committed Dependencies: ragnar, Ollama nomic-embed-text model See /lit-search skill full recipe

title: mc
text: ragnar_store_connect() ragnar_retrieve() — returns chunks source file attribution.

title: mc
text: NEVER write abstracts manually — CrossRef abstract, leave blank NEVER cite specific numbers without verifying source PDF via ragnar search NEVER paraphrase equations — copy exact notation cite page/section

title: mc
text: SR&ED tracking integrates New Graph Environment’s development workflows.

title: mc
text: SRED-eligible work across NGE falls single continuous project: Dynamic GIS-based Data Processing Reporting Framework Field: Software Engineering (2.02.09) Start date: May 2022 Fiscal year: May 1 – April 30 Consultant: Boast Capital (prepares final technical report) fragment work separate claims. fiscal year’s work structured iterations within one project. Internal tracking (experiment numbers sred-2025-2026) maps iterations — Boast assembles final narrative.

title: mc
text: Use Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N commit messages work SRED-eligible.

title: mc
text: Tag hours sred_ref field linking relevant sred-2025-2026 issue number.

title: mc
text: Link SRED-eligible issues tracking repo: Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N

title: mc
text: Eligible (systematic investigation overcome technological uncertainty): - Building tools/functions don’t exist standard practice - Prototyping new integrations systems (GIS ↔︎ reporting ↔︎ field collection) - Testing whether approach works documenting /didn’t - Iterating failed approaches new hypotheses eligible: - Standard configuration known tools - Routine bug fixes working systems - Writing reports using framework (’s service delivery) test: “try something weren’t sure work, learn something attempt?” yes, ’s likely eligible.

title: Tables in Emails
text: mc_md_render() converts single markdown file HTML. need R-generated table middle email? can’t put R code plain .md file.

title: Tables in Emails
text: mc_compose() takes mix markdown files, HTML strings, kable objects stitches one email body. argument resolved order: .md file — header --- stripped, body converted HTML kable/kableExtra object — converted HTML via .character() HTML string — passed -<table>, <th>, <td> elements get border padding styles injected automatically Gmail compatibility.
code:
library(mc) df <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000) ) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if the numbers look right.

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "Planting site summary")

title: Tables in Emails
text: reliable option. Clean HTML, minimal styling, works everywhere. mc_compose() adds border padding inline styles every cell.
code: df <- data.frame(   Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"),   Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000),   Status = c("Confirmed", "Pending", "Pending") )  knitr::kable(df, format = "html")

title: Tables in Emails
text: colors, bold headers, highlighted rows, use kableExtra::row_spec() kableExtra::column_spec(). add inline styles survive Gmail’s CSS stripping. Avoid bootstrap_options like "striped" "hover" — rely CSS classes <style> block Gmail strips. Use row_spec() explicit background colors instead:
code: library(kableExtra)  kbl(df, format = "html") |>   kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |>   row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5") |>   column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) |>   column_spec(2, color = "#2c7bb6") kbl(df, format = "html") |>   kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |>   row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5") |>   row_spec(seq(2, nrow(df), 2), background = "#f9f9f9")

title: Tables in Emails
text: Large tables can wrapped scrollable <div> using mc_scroll(). Gmail preserves overflow CSS scroll email client.

title: Tables in Emails
code: wide_df <- data.frame(   Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"),   Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000),   Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026"),   Latitude = c(53.92, 55.34, 54.38),   Longitude = c(-124.02, -122.97, -127.17),   BEC_Zone = c("SBSdw3", "SBSmk1", "SBSdk"),   Elevation_m = c(680, 700, 520),   Stock = c("Local", "Transfer", "Transfer"),   Nursery = c("PRT Vernon", "PRT Vernon", "PRT Vernon"),   Crew = c("Alpine Reforestation", "TBD", "TBD"),   Access = c("FSR 200", "FSR 440", "Morice West"),   Prep = c("Complete", "Scheduled", "Not started"),   Soil = c("Silty clay", "Sandy loam", "Clay loam"),   Moisture = c("Subhygric", "Mesic", "Hygric"),   Permit = c("Approved", "In review", "Not submitted") )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(wide_df, format = "html"), direction = "wide"))

title: Tables in Emails
code: set.seed(42) sites <- c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena", "Fraser", "Bulkley",            "Stellako", "Endako", "Stuart", "Salmon", "Bowron") long_df <- data.frame(   Row = 1:50,   Site = rep(sites, 5),   Plugs = sample(100:500, 50, replace = TRUE),   Survival_Pct = round(runif(50, 60, 98), 1),   Height_cm = round(runif(50, 15, 85), 0) )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(long_df, format = "html"), direction = "long"))

title: Tables in Emails
text: default max_height vertical scrolling "400px". Adjust mc_scroll(..., max_height = "600px").
code: set.seed(42) both_df <- data.frame(   Row = 1:30,   Site = rep(sites, 3),   Plugs = sample(100:500, 30, replace = TRUE),   Survival = round(runif(30, 60, 98), 1),   Height_cm = round(runif(30, 15, 85), 0),   BEC_Zone = rep(c("SBSdw3", "SBSmk1", "SBSdk", "SBSwk1", "SBSmc2",                    "SBSdw1", "SBSwk3", "SBSdk", "SBSmw", "SBSdw2"), 3),   Elevation = sample(400:900, 30, replace = TRUE),   Lat = round(runif(30, 53, 56), 4),   Lon = round(runif(30, -128, -122), 4),   Source = rep(c("Local", "Transfer"), 15),   Soil = rep(c("Silty clay", "Sandy loam", "Clay loam", "Silt loam", "Gravel"), 6),   Moisture = rep(c("Subhygric", "Mesic", "Hygric", "Subxeric", "Mesic"), 6),   Fish = rep(c("Yes", "Yes", "No", "Yes", "No"), 6),   Permit = rep(c("Approved", "In review", "Not submitted", "Approved", "Pending"), 6) )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(both_df, format = "html"), direction = "both"))

title: Tables in Emails
code:
library(mc) sites <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000), Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026") ) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

Here's the current planting plan:

", kableExtra::kbl(sites, format = "html") |> kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |> kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5"), "

Total: 10,000 plugs across three sites.

Does this match what the nursery has available?

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan — cottonwood plugs")

title: Tables in Emails
text: Use send_at send email later. works mc_compose() mc_scroll() — build body now, send want. macOS, caffeinate prevents machine sleeping email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. machine sleep send window, 5-minute grace period applies — past send skipped prevent stale emails firing. scheduled send outcomes (sent, skipped, failed) logged ~/.mc/send_log.txt trigger macOS desktop notification.
code:
library(mc) sites <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000), Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026") ) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

Here's the planting plan with a scrollable table:

", mc_scroll( kableExtra::kbl(sites, format = "html") |> kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |> kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5"), direction = "both" ), "

Let me know if the numbers look right.

" ) # Send in 30 minutes proc <- mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan", send_at = 30) # Or at a specific time mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan", send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:00:00")) # Check or cancel proc$is_alive() proc$kill()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Allan Irvine. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {mc: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery},   author = {Allan Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.1},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/mc}, }

what: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Mail Composer. Three lines draft email. Compose, draft, send emails markdown files via Gmail API. Wraps gmailr eliminate 80 lines boilerplate every email script repeats.

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/mc")

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Set default sender address ~/.Rprofile: via environment variable ~/.Renviron: authenticate per machine:
code: options(mc.from = "you@example.com") MC_FROM=you@example.com mc_auth()

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Write email body markdown. Everything --- separator human-readable envelope (notes author) stripped mc_md_render() conversion. Recipients, subject, envelope fields set R parameters mc_send(). Send three lines: creates Gmail draft HTML formatting standard signature appended. Authentication happens automatically via cached OAuth tokens (run mc_auth() per machine set ). ’re ready send real:
code: # Email to Brandon - Cottonwood  **Subject:** Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting **To:** brandon@example.com  ---  Hi Brandon,  Quick question about the cottonwood plugs.  Thanks, Al library(mc) mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting") mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Need R-generated table email? Use mc_compose() mix markdown files, HTML, kable/kableExtra objects: Large tables? Wrap mc_scroll() horizontal/vertical scrolling: See vignette("tables--emails") kableExtra styling scrolling examples.
code:
df <- data.frame(Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000)) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if this looks right.

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "Planting plan") mc_compose( "

Full dataset:

", mc_scroll(knitr::kable(big_df, format = "html"), direction = "both") )

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Full function documentation examples: newgraphenvironment.github.io/mc

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Reply existing conversation: Note: gm_create_draft() support thread_id. Drafts always standalone. Use draft = FALSE send directly thread, send draft manually Gmail UI (Gmail match subject line starts “Re:”).
code: # Find the thread mc_thread_find("from:brandon subject:cottonwood")  # Read the conversation to review context mc_thread_read("19c05f0a98188c91")  # Include drafts in the thread (adds a status column: "sent" / "draft") mc_thread_read("19c05f0a98188c91", drafts = TRUE)  # Send into it mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Send email later send_at — either minutes now specific time: macOS, caffeinate keeps machine awake email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. machine sleeps send window (e.g., power loss), 5-minute grace period applies — past , send skipped prevent stale emails. Outcomes logged ~/.mc/send_log.txt trigger macOS desktop notification success, skip, failure.
code: # Send in 10 minutes proc <- mc_send("draft.md",                 to = "brandon@example.com",                 subject = "Cottonwood plugs",                 send_at = 10)  # Send at a specific time mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:11:00"))  # Check status or cancel proc$is_alive() proc$kill()

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Send preview: Test mode redirects address, strips CC/BCC, ignores thread_id.
code: mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: MIT

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
code: mc_auth(email = default_from())

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: email Email address authenticate . Default uses getOption("mc."), MC_FROM environment variable. Errors neither set.

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect authenticating.

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth

what: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: Combines markdown files, HTML strings, kable/kableExtra table objects single HTML email body. Use need R-generated tables dynamic content mixed prose.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
code: mc_compose(..., sig = TRUE, sig_path = NULL)

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: ... Parts compose, order. can : Markdown file path .md file path. header --- stripped (present) body converted HTML. HTML string character string raw HTML, passed -. kable/kableExtra object Output knitr::kable() kableExtra functions. Converted character automatically. sig Logical. Append signature? Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: character string HTML ready html argument mc_send().

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: tables look good email: Use knitr::kable() clean, simple tables Use kableExtra::row_spec() kableExtra::column_spec() inline styling (colors, bold, backgrounds) Avoid bootstrap_options like "striped" — CSS classes stripped Gmail Wrap large tables mc_scroll() horizontal/vertical scrolling mc_compose() automatically adds border padding inline styles <table>, <th>, <td> elements Gmail compatibility.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
code:
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Prose + table + more prose df <- data.frame(Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000)) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "communications/closing.md" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "someone@example.com", subject = "Update") # Inline markdown (no file needed) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if this looks right.

" ) } # }

what: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Reads markdown file, strips everything first --- separator (header metadata), converts remaining markdown HTML, inlines table styles Gmail compatibility, appends New Graph signature.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
code: mc_md_render(path, sig = TRUE, sig_path = NULL)

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: path Path markdown draft file. sig Logical. Append signature? Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature. Ignored sig = FALSE.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: character string HTML ready mc_send().

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Gmail strips <style> blocks HTML emails. function adds inline styles <table>, <th>, <td> elements tables render correctly. markdown file follow compost template format:   Everything including --- line stripped. Everything converted HTML.
code: # Email to Recipient - Topic  **Subject:** ...  **To:** ...  ---  Hi Name,  Body text here.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ html <- mc_md_render("communications/20260222_brandon_cottonwood_draft.md") cat(html) } # }

text: character string HTML table inside scrollable div.

Here's the data:

what: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: main function. Reads markdown draft, renders HTML, builds MIME message, either creates Gmail draft sends immediately.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: mc_send(   path = NULL,   to,   subject,   cc = NULL,   bcc = NULL,   from = default_from(),   thread_id = NULL,   draft = TRUE,   test = FALSE,   sig = TRUE,   sig_path = NULL,   attachments = NULL,   html = NULL,   send_at = NULL )

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: path Path markdown draft file. Passed mc_md_render(). Recipient email address (character string vector). subject Email subject line. cc Optional CC recipients (character vector). Default NULL. bcc Optional BCC recipients (character vector). Default NULL. Sender address. Default uses getOption("mc."), MC_FROM environment variable. Errors neither set. thread_id Gmail thread ID reply . Default NULL (new thread). Use mc_thread_find() look thread IDs. draft Logical. TRUE (default), create Gmail draft. FALSE, send immediately. test Logical. TRUE, override (send self) ignore cc thread_id. Default FALSE. sig Logical. Append signature? Passed mc_md_render(). Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature. Passed mc_md_render(). Ignored sig = FALSE html provided. attachments Optional character vector file paths attach. file attached via gmailr::gm_attach_file(). Default NULL. html Optional pre-rendered HTML body. provided, path ignored HTML used directly. send_at Schedule email later. Either POSIXct datetime numeric number minutes now. Default NULL (send/draft immediately). set, draft forced FALSE email sent background R process via callr::r_bg(). Requires callr package.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: send_at NULL, invisible NULL. send_at set, returns callr::r_bg() process handle invisibly. Use $is_alive() check status $kill() cancel.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Authenticates automatically active Gmail session detected.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Gmail's gm_create_draft() support thread_id. draft = TRUE thread_id set, mc_send() issues warning draft appear thread manually sent Gmail UI. Set subject "Re: Original Subject" Gmail's thread-matching heuristic can place correctly. draft = FALSE thread_id set, message sent directly thread via gm_send_message(thread_id = ...).

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: test = TRUE sends , strips CC, ignores thread_id prevent accidental sends real threads development.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: send_at runs background R process machine. macOS, caffeinate used prevent idle sleep machine stays awake email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. Laptop powered — sends time (caffeinate prevents sleep) Laptop powered — process dies, email never sends caffeinate bypassed machine sleeps send window, 5-minute grace period applies. Past , send skipped prevent stale emails firing unexpectedly.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a draft (safe default) mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs")  # Send into an existing thread mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)  # Test mode — sends to self mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE)  # Send in 10 minutes proc <- mc_send("communications/draft.md",                 to = "brandon@example.com",                 subject = "Cottonwood plugs",                 send_at = 10) proc$is_alive()  # check if still waiting proc$kill()      # cancel  # Send at a specific time mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:11:00"))  # Attach files mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Planting plan",         attachments = c("data/plan.xlsx", "fig/map.pdf")) } # }

what: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: Reads signature HTML file. Defaults standard New Graph signature bundled inst/sig/signature.html. Pass custom path use different signature.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
code: mc_sig(path = NULL)

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: path Path signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: character string HTML.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cat(mc_sig()) cat(mc_sig("path/to/custom_sig.html")) } # }

what: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: Searches Gmail messages returns matching thread IDs. Useful finding thread_id pass mc_send() replying existing conversation.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
code: mc_thread_find(query, n = 5)

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: query Gmail search query. Supports syntax Gmail search box (e.g., ":brandon subject:cottonwood"). n Maximum number results. Default 5.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: data frame columns thread_id, , subject, date, sorted recent first.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_thread_find("from:brandon.geldart subject:cottonwood") mc_thread_find("from:brandon newer_than:7d") } # }

what: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: Fetches thread ID returns message's sender, date, subject, plain-text body. Useful reviewing conversation composing follow-mc_send().

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
code: mc_thread_read(thread_id, drafts = FALSE)

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: thread_id Gmail thread ID (mc_thread_find()). drafts Logical. TRUE, also include draft messages output add status column ("sent" "draft"). Default FALSE backwards compatibility.

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: data frame columns , date, subject, body, ordered oldest newest. drafts = TRUE, additional status column included.

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_thread_find("from:brandon subject:cottonwood") mc_thread_read("19adb18351867c34") mc_thread_read("19adb18351867c34", drafts = TRUE) } # }

title: mc 0.2.1
text: Add drafts parameter mc_thread_read() — includes draft messages status column ("sent" / "draft").

title: mc 0.2.0
text: Add attachments parameter mc_send() file attachments. Remove hardcoded fallback email default_from() — now errors options(mc.) MC_FROM env var unset. Update CLAUDE.md latest soul conventions.

title: mc 0.1.0
text: First stable release: compose, draft, send emails markdown via Gmail API. Core functions: mc_send(), mc_compose(), mc_md_render(), mc_scroll(), mc_sig(). Thread support: mc_thread_find(), mc_thread_read(). Scheduled send send_at macOS caffeinate integration. Test mode redirects sender safe previewing.
